ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT OWENS-ILLINOIS, INC.'S JOINDER IN DEFENDANT HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC., SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO THE BENDIX CORPORATIONS MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT OWENS-ILLINOIS, INC.'S JOINDER IN HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC.'S MOTION IS DENIED AS UNTIMELY. THE UNDERLYING MOTION IS OFF CALENDAR AND DEFENDANT FAILED TO PROVIDE SUFFICIENT NOTICE OF ITS JOINDER AS REQUIRED BY C.C.P. 1005(B) AND 1010.6. THE DENIAL IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT BRINGING ITS OWN PROPERLY-NOTICED MOTION. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTION BASED ON TIMELINESS IS SUSTAINED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

Defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Oct-30-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.'s motion to dismiss for forum non conveniens.

APPEARANCES: Oren P. Noah, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 415/898-1555 for pltfs & opposing parties; no appearance by moving party defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.

RULING: The Court vacated its tentative ruling and took the matter off calendar per request of the moving party. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Not Reported (503/TLJ)

Defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Oct-15-2014 Continued To Oct-30-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.'s motion to dismiss on the grounds of forum non conveniens is continued to October 30, 2014 per the October 8, 2014 stipulation and order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Sep-11-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Honeywell International Inc.'s motion to dismiss or stay for forum non conveniens is off calendar at the moving party's request. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

Plaintiffs' Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Continued From Sep-10-2014)
Off Calendar Sep-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Charles Shall and Ursula Shall
Lauren N. Peebles of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
Page C. Perry of BASSI EDLIN HUIE & BLUM (415-403-4429), appearing for defendant / opposing party J. T. Thorpe & Son, Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE

After discussion, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as revised: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Trial is January 8, 2015 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is January 7, 2015. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice dictated by the C.C.P., unless plaintiffs stipulate otherwise. Time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. The medicare and social security authorization must be provided to the designated defense counsel by October 31, 2014. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than October 10, 2014. Responses to the demand are due no later than October 17, 2014. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by December 29, 2014 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on January 5, 2015. Fact discovery cut-off date is December 19, 2014. Expert discovery cut-off date is January 2, 2015. No later than September 25, 2014 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than September 25, 2014 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on November 19, 2014 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on January 8, 2015 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by January 6, 2015 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.S Joinder In Defendant Honeywell International Inc., Successor-In-Interest To The Bendix Corporations Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Sep-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Charles Shall and Ursula Shall

RULING:
DEFENDANT OWENS-ILLINOIS, INC.'S JOINDER IN DEFENDANT HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC., SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO THE BENDIX CORPORATIONS MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.'s joinder in Honeywell International Inc.'s motion to dismiss or stay for forum non conveniens is denied as untimely. The underlying motion is off calendar and defendant failed to provide sufficient notice of its joinder as required by C.C.P. 1005(b) and 1010.6. The denial is without prejudice to defendant bringing its own properly-noticed motion. Plaintiffs' objection based on timeliness is sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff

MINI MINUTES:
